questions.question: Who is a consumer?
questions.correct_answer: A person who buys goods or services for personal use
questions.explanation: A consumer is someone who purchases goods or services for personal consumption and not for resale.

questions.question: What is consumer exploitation?
questions.correct_answer: Unfair treatment of consumers by sellers
questions.explanation: Consumer exploitation includes overcharging, selling defective goods, adulteration, and misleading advertisements.

questions.question: Name one consumer right.
questions.explanation: The Right to Information ensures that consumers get complete details about the product such as price, ingredients and expiry date.

questions.question: Which right protects consumers against hazardous goods?
questions.explanation: The Right to Safety protects consumers from goods and services that may be harmful to health or life.

questions.question: What does the Right to Seek Redressal mean?
questions.correct_answer: Right to get compensation for unfair practices
questions.explanation: Consumers can approach consumer courts to seek compensation for defective goods or unfair trade practices.

questions.question: What is the Consumer Protection Act?
questions.correct_answer: A law to protect consumer rights
questions.explanation: The Consumer Protection Act was enacted to safeguard consumers from exploitation and provide legal remedies.

questions.question: Name the three levels of consumer courts.
questions.explanation: Consumer disputes are resolved at District Forum, State Commission and National Commission levels.

questions.question: What does ISI mark indicate?
questions.explanation: ISI mark indicates that the product meets the quality standards set by the Bureau of Indian Standards.

questions.question: Why is a bill important while purchasing goods?
questions.correct_answer: It acts as proof of purchase
questions.explanation: A bill is necessary to file a complaint in consumer court in case of defective goods or services.

questions.question: What is a misleading advertisement?
questions.correct_answer: An advertisement giving false information about a product
questions.explanation: Misleading advertisements exaggerate or provide false claims about a product to attract consumers.

notes.title: Consumer Rights
notes.content: Important consumer rights include Right to Safety, Right to Information, Right to Choose, Right to Seek Redressal and Right to Consumer Education.

notes.title: Unfair Trade Practices
notes.content: Unfair trade practices include adulteration, underweight goods, misleading advertisements and overcharging.

notes.title: Consumer Courts
notes.content: Consumer courts provide a three-tier redressal system to resolve consumer complaints efficiently.

notes.title: Quality Certification Marks
notes.content: ISI, Agmark, Hallmark and FSSAI marks ensure product quality and safety.

notes.title: Consumer Awareness
notes.content: Consumer awareness is essential to prevent exploitation and ensure fair market practices.

facts.fact: The Consumer Protection Act was first passed in 1986 and later amended.
Consumers have both rights and responsibilities in the marketplace.
Agmark is used for agricultural products.
Hallmark certification ensures purity of gold jewellery.
Consumer awareness campaigns are often conducted by the government and NGOs.
